reasoning: Initial observation suggests a direct relationship between mass, density, volume, and a scaling constant. To confirm the role of k, I will increase the density and observe the effect on mass.
Increasing the density while keeping volume and k constant increased the mass as expected. Now, I will increase the volume to see if it affects the mass.

The mass has doubled when the volume was doubled, suggesting a linear relationship. The value of k remains unchanged, indicating it is a multiplicative factor. The initial observation and subsequent changes support the equation mass = density * volume * k.
